verses.text: After fourteen years I went up; Titus, a Greek, was 'not compelled to be circumcised.' Liberty upheld. The Gentile was not bound to the law.
After fourteen years I went up; Titus, a Greek, was 'not compelled to be circumcised.' Liberty upheld. The Gentile was not bound to the law.
After fourteen years I went up; Titus, a Greek, was 'not compelled to be circumcised.' Liberty upheld. The Gentile was not bound to the law.
False brethren came 'to spy out our liberty'; we yielded not, 'that the truth... might continue.' Firm. He would not give ground to legalism.
False brethren came 'to spy out our liberty'; we yielded not, 'that the truth... might continue.' Firm. He would not give ground to legalism.
The apostles added nothing to me; the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed to me. Equal calling. His apostleship to the Gentiles was owned.
The apostles added nothing to me; the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed to me. Equal calling. His apostleship to the Gentiles was owned.
The apostles added nothing to me; the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed to me. Equal calling. His apostleship to the Gentiles was owned.
James, Cephas, and John gave 'the right hands of fellowship'; only remember the poor. Mutual recognition. They confirmed his work and ministry.
James, Cephas, and John gave 'the right hands of fellowship'; only remember the poor. Mutual recognition. They confirmed his work and ministry.
I withstood Peter at Antioch, for he withdrew from the Gentiles, fearing the Jews. Bold rebuke. Even an apostle erred and was corrected.
I withstood Peter at Antioch, for he withdrew from the Gentiles, fearing the Jews. Bold rebuke. Even an apostle erred and was corrected.
I withstood Peter at Antioch, for he withdrew from the Gentiles, fearing the Jews. Bold rebuke. Even an apostle erred and was corrected.
A man is 'not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ.' The great truth. Justification is by faith, not works.
A man is 'not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ.' The great truth. Justification is by faith, not works.
A man is 'not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ.' The great truth. Justification is by faith, not works.
I through the law am 'dead to the law, that I might live unto God.' Free from law. The law's claim is ended for the believer.
I through the law am 'dead to the law, that I might live unto God.' Free from law. The law's claim is ended for the believer.
I through the law am 'dead to the law, that I might live unto God.' Free from law. The law's claim is ended for the believer.
'I am crucified with Christ... I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.' New life. Christ lives in the believer by faith.
'I am crucified with Christ... I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.' New life. Christ lives in the believer by faith.
